skill.name: architect-review
skill.description: Reviews software architecture, service boundaries, scalability, resilience, security posture, and maintainability for complex systems.
skill.summary: Architecture review guidance for distributed systems, clean architecture, and technical decision making.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md, but review found these are terminology false positives in architecture guidance. No scripts, executable code, network access, filesystem access, environment access, or prompt injection attempts were found.

content.user_title: Review Architecture Decisions With Expert Guidance
content.value_statement: Complex systems can drift into fragile service boundaries, hidden coupling, and unclear tradeoffs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured architecture reviews.
actual_capabilities: Reviews system designs for scalability, resilience, and maintainability concerns.
Evaluates microservice boundaries, bounded contexts, and domain modeling choices.
Assesses architecture patterns such as clean architecture, event sourcing, CQRS, and service mesh design.
Identifies architectural violations, anti-patterns, technical debt, and missing validation plans.
Recommends tradeoffs, next steps, and documentation such as architecture decision records.
limitations: It does not execute tests, deploy infrastructure, or inspect systems without supplied context.
It depends on accurate requirements, diagrams, code changes, and operational constraints from the user.
It is not a substitute for security, compliance, or reliability sign-off by accountable teams.
Small local code changes may not need this architecture-level review.

use_cases.title: Review a New Service Design
use_cases.description: Evaluate service boundaries, data ownership, API contracts, and failure modes before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Assess a Platform Migration
use_cases.description: Compare architecture tradeoffs for moving workloads to Kubernetes, serverless, or a cloud-native platform.

use_cases.title: Prepare Architecture Governance
use_cases.description: Turn design concerns into decisions, risks, validation tasks, and architecture decision records.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Architecture Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a proposed design and need a structured first review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this architecture proposal. Focus on scalability, maintainability, service boundaries, resilience, and security assumptions. Summarize risks and recommend next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Microservice Boundary Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when splitting or changing service ownership.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these microservice boundaries against domain-driven design principles. Identify coupling, shared data risks, ownership gaps, and better bounded context options.

prompt_templates.title: Event-Driven Design Assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when introducing events, queues, CQRS, or event sourcing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this event-driven architecture. Review event ownership, ordering, idempotency, failure handling, observability, replay needs, and consistency tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Decision Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when preparing an architecture decision record for senior review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this architecture decision. Challenge the assumptions, compare alternatives, identify validation work, and produce a concise decision record outline.

output_examples.input: Review a proposal to split billing, subscriptions, and invoicing into separate services.
output: The main concern is shared ownership of customer account state.
Billing and invoicing can be separate if account status changes are published through stable domain events.
Validate failure handling for retries, duplicate events, and delayed subscription changes before implementation.

output_examples.input: Assess whether to add event sourcing to an order management system.
output: Event sourcing may improve auditability, but it adds operational and modeling complexity.
The design needs clear event versioning, snapshot strategy, replay controls, and support ownership.
Use it only for aggregates where history is a core product or compliance requirement.

output_examples.input: Review a Kubernetes migration plan for a monolithic application.
output: The migration improves deployment consistency, but it does not remove application coupling.
Start with observability, readiness checks, resource limits, and rollback procedures.
Delay service extraction until module boundaries and data ownership are validated.

best_practices: Provide architecture diagrams, requirements, constraints, and known failure modes before asking for review.
Ask for tradeoffs and validation steps, not only a pass or fail decision.
Record accepted decisions, rejected alternatives, assumptions, and follow-up owners.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to approve major architecture changes without system context.
Treating pattern names as proof that a design is maintainable or scalable.
Skipping validation for reliability, security, observability, and deployment risks.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews architecture decisions, service boundaries, distributed system patterns, scalability, resilience, maintainability, and technical debt.

faq.question: Can it review code changes?
faq.answer: Yes, when the changes affect architecture, module boundaries, data ownership, deployment shape, or system qualities.

faq.question: Does it replace a security review?
faq.answer: No. It can identify security architecture concerns, but dedicated security review remains necessary for high-risk systems.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide goals, constraints, diagrams, service responsibilities, data flows, risks, and the decision you need to make.

faq.question: Is it useful for monoliths?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review modular boundaries, dependency direction, deployment constraints, and migration options for monolithic systems.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
